10.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
10.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

10.title: Observability
10.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

12.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
12.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

15.title: Architecture Overview
15.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

17.title: Deployment with Helm
17.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

23.title: Authentication
23.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

23.title: Deployment with Helm
23.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

23.title: A2A Registry
23.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

25.title: Observability
25.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

26.title: ADL CLI
26.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

30.title: Channels
30.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

30.title: Deployment with Helm
30.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

36.title: ADL CLI
36.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

50.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
50.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

for.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
for.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

for.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
for.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

for.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
for.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

for.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
for.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

for.title: Observability
for.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

for.title: Kubernetes Operator
for.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

for.title: A2A Registry
for.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

for.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
for.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

for.title: Supported Providers
for.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

for.title: Troubleshooting
for.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

it.title: A2A Debugger
it.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

it.title: ADL CLI
it.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

it.title: API Reference
it.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

it.title: Channels
it.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

it.title: Getting Started
it.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

it.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

installation.title: ADL CLI
installation.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

installation.title: API Reference
installation.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

installation.title: Authentication
installation.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

installation.title: Inference Gateway CLI
installation.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

installation.title: Deployment with Helm
installation.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

install.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

from.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

from.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
from.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

from.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
from.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

from.title: Observability
from.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

from.title: Kubernetes Operator
from.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

from.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
from.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

from.title: Troubleshooting
from.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

source.title: A2A Debugger
source.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

source.title: ADL CLI
source.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

source.title: Inference Gateway CLI
source.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

source.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
source.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

source.title: Kubernetes Operator

Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

source.title: A2A Registry
source.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

quick.title: Channels
quick.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

get.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

get.title: Channels
get.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

get.title: Inference Gateway CLI
get.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

get.title: Deployment with Helm
get.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

get.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
get.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

get.title: Kubernetes Operator
get.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

get.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
get.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

common.title: A2A Debugger
common.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

common.title: Authentication
common.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

common.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
common.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

an.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
an.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

an.title: ADL CLI
an.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

an.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
an.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

output.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

output.title: ADL CLI
output.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

output.title: Authentication
output.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

output.title: Channels
output.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

running.title: API Reference
running.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

running.title: Architecture Overview
running.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

running.title: Authentication
running.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

running.title: Channels
running.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

running.title: Deployment with Helm
running.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

running.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
running.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

running.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
running.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

running.title: A2A Registry
running.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

running.title: Troubleshooting
running.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. the.title: Observability
the.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

agenttoagent.title: A2A Debugger
agenttoagent.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

agenttoagent.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
agenttoagent.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

agenttoagent.title: ADL CLI
agenttoagent.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

agenttoagent.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
agenttoagent.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

agenttoagent.title: A2A Registry
agenttoagent.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

agenttoagent.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
agenttoagent.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

servers.title: A2A Debugger
servers.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

servers.title: ADL CLI
servers.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

servers.title: Inference Gateway CLI
servers.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

servers.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
servers.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

servers.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
servers.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

servers.title: Kubernetes Operator
servers.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

servers.title: Troubleshooting
servers.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

small.title: A2A Debugger
small.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

small.title: Channels
small.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

writing.title: A2A Debugger
writing.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

writing.title: A2A Registry
writing.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

client.title: A2A Debugger

It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

client.title: Architecture Overview
client.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

client.title: Authentication
client.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

client.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
client.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

client.title: Kubernetes Operator
client.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

client.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
client.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

client.title: Troubleshooting

note.title: Channels
note.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

https.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

https.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
https.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

https.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
https.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

https.title: Kubernetes Operator
https.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

https.title: A2A Registry
https.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

https.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
https.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

https.title: Supported Providers
https.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

https.title: Troubleshooting
https.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

github.title: A2A Debugger
github.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

github.title: ADL CLI
github.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

github.title: Authentication
github.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

github.title: Channels
github.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

github.title: Inference Gateway CLI
github.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

github.title: Deployment with Helm
github.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

github.title: Examples
github.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

or.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

or.title: ADL CLI
or.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

or.title: API Reference
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

or.title: Authentication
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

or.title: Channels
or.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

or.title: Inference Gateway CLI
or.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

or.title: Deployment with Helm
or.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

or.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
or.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

or.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

or.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

or.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
or.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

or.title: Observability
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

or.title: Kubernetes Operator
or.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

or.title: A2A Registry
or.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

or.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

or.title: Supported Providers
or.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

final.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
final.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

with.title: A2A Debugger

with.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
with.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

with.title: ADL CLI
with.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

with.title: API Reference
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

with.title: Architecture Overview
with.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

with.title: Authentication
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

with.title: Channels
with.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

with.title: Inference Gateway CLI
with.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

with.title: Configuration
with.excerpt: import ConfigTable from '../components/ConfigTable';

with.title: Deployment with Helm
with.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

with.title: Examples
with.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

with.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
with.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

with.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

with.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

with.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
with.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

with.title: Observability
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

with.title: Kubernetes Operator
with.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

with.title: A2A Registry
with.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

with.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

with.title: Supported Providers
with.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

with.title: Troubleshooting
with.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

inference.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

inference.title: Supported Providers
inference.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

inference.title: Troubleshooting
inference.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

gateway.title: A2A Debugger
gateway.excerpt: The **A2A Debugger** is the ultimate troubleshooting and inspection tool for [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) servers. It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

gateway.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
gateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

gateway.title: ADL CLI
gateway.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

gateway.title: API Reference
gateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

gateway.title: Architecture Overview
gateway.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

gateway.title: Authentication
gateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

gateway.title: Channels
gateway.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

gateway.title: Inference Gateway CLI
gateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

gateway.title: Configuration
gateway.excerpt: import ConfigTable from '../components/ConfigTable';

gateway.title: Deployment with Helm
gateway.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

gateway.title: Examples
gateway.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

gateway.title: Getting Started
gateway.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

gateway.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
gateway.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

gateway.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
gateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

gateway.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
gateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

gateway.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
gateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

gateway.title: Observability
gateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

gateway.title: Kubernetes Operator
gateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

git.title: Authentication
git.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

git.title: Channels
git.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

git.title: Inference Gateway CLI
git.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

git.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
git.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

git.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
git.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

set.title: Authentication
set.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

set.title: Deployment with Helm
set.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

set.title: Examples
set.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

set.title: Getting Started
set.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

set.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
set.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

set.title: Observability
set.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

set.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
set.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

set.title: Troubleshooting
set.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

one.title: API Reference
one.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

one.title: Channels
one.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

one.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
one.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

one.title: Kubernetes Operator
one.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

operations.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
operations.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

operations.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
operations.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

agentcard.title: A2A Registry
agentcard.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

fetch.title: ADL CLI
fetch.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

key.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

It's a small Go CLI (`a2a`) that connects to any A2A-compatible agent, lists and streams tasks, replays conversation histories, and inspects agent cards - so you can debug agent behaviour without writing throwaway client code.

context.title: Channels
context.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

context.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
context.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

context.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
context.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

context.title: Kubernetes Operator
context.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

context.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
context.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

context.title: Troubleshooting
context.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

message.title: Inference Gateway CLI
message.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

message.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
message.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

response.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
response.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

response.title: API Reference
response.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

response.title: Authentication
response.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

works.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

using.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
using.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

using.title: API Reference
using.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

using.title: Authentication
using.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

using.title: Deployment with Helm
using.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

using.title: Examples
using.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

using.title: Getting Started
using.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

using.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
using.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

using.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
using.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

using.title: Supported Providers
using.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

started.title: Channels
started.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

initialize.title: ADL CLI
initialize.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

initialize.title: Inference Gateway CLI

interactive.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
interactive.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

chat.title: ADL CLI

It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

chat.title: API Reference
chat.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

chat.title: Channels
chat.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

chat.title: Inference Gateway CLI
chat.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

chat.title: Examples
chat.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

chat.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
chat.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

chat.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
chat.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

chat.title: Kubernetes Operator
chat.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

chat.title: A2A Registry
chat.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

chat.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
chat.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

chat.title: Troubleshooting
chat.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

google.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

google.title: ADL CLI
google.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

google.title: Supported Providers
google.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

calendar.title: A2A Registry
calendar.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

creating.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
creating.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

this.title: Channels
this.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

feature.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
feature.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

allows.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
allows.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

access.title: Authentication
access.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

of.title: API Reference

of.title: Architecture Overview

of.title: Authentication
of.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

of.title: Channels

of.title: Examples
of.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

of.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
of.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

of.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
of.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

of.title: Observability
of.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

of.title: Kubernetes Operator
of.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

of.title: Troubleshooting
of.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

services.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
services.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents.
