services.title: ADL CLI
services.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

services.title: Deployment with Helm

services.title: A2A Registry
services.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

their.title: Supported Providers
their.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

multiagent.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
multiagent.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

coordination.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
coordination.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

provides.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
provides.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

provides.title: ADL CLI
provides.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

provides.title: API Reference
provides.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

provides.title: Architecture Overview
provides.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

provides.title: Authentication
provides.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

provides.title: Inference Gateway CLI
provides.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

provides.title: Examples
provides.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

provides.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
provides.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

provides.title: Supported Providers
provides.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

distributed.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
distributed.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

distributed.title: Observability
distributed.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

distributed.title: Kubernetes Operator
distributed.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

architecture.title: Architecture Overview

architecture.title: Inference Gateway CLI

This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

run.title: ADL CLI
run.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

run.title: Architecture Overview
run.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

run.title: Channels
run.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

run.title: Inference Gateway CLI
run.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

run.title: Deployment with Helm
run.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

run.title: Getting Started
run.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

run.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
run.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

run.title: A2A Registry
run.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

run.title: Troubleshooting
run.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

registry.title: Deployment with Helm
registry.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

registry.title: A2A Registry
registry.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

results.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
results.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

results.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
results.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

system.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
system.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

all.title: ADL CLI
all.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

all.title: API Reference
all.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

all.title: Authentication
all.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

all.title: Channels
all.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

all.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
all.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

managing.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

browse.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
browse.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

browse.title: A2A Registry
browse.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

hosted.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
hosted.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

hosted.title: A2A Registry

It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

inferencegateway.title: Agent-To-Agent (A2A) Integration
inferencegateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Agent-To-Agent (A2A)** integration, enabling Large Language Models (LLMs) to seamlessly coordinate with external specialized agents. This powerful feature allows LLMs to access and utilize a wide range of external tools and services through standardized agent interfaces.

inferencegateway.title: ADL CLI
inferencegateway.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

inferencegateway.title: Architecture Overview
inferencegateway.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

inferencegateway.title: Authentication
inferencegateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

inferencegateway.title: Deployment with Helm
inferencegateway.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

inferencegateway.title: Examples
inferencegateway.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

inferencegateway.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
inferencegateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

inferencegateway.title: Observability
inferencegateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

inferencegateway.title: Kubernetes Operator
inferencegateway.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

inferencegateway.title: A2A Registry
inferencegateway.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

inferencegateway.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
inferencegateway.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

kubernetes.title: ADL CLI
kubernetes.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

kubernetes.title: Architecture Overview
kubernetes.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

kubernetes.title: Authentication
kubernetes.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

kubernetes.title: Deployment with Helm
kubernetes.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

kubernetes.title: Examples
kubernetes.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

kubernetes.title: Getting Started
kubernetes.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

kubernetes.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
kubernetes.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

kubernetes.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
kubernetes.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

kubernetes.title: Observability
kubernetes.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

kubernetes.title: Kubernetes Operator
kubernetes.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

fullfeatured.title: ADL CLI

reference.title: ADL CLI
reference.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

reference.title: API Reference
reference.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

reference.title: Channels
reference.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

reference.title: Inference Gateway CLI
reference.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

reference.title: Observability
reference.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

reference.title: A2A Registry
reference.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

reference.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
reference.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

reference.title: Troubleshooting
reference.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

actions.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
actions.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

provider.title: API Reference
provider.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

provider.title: Architecture Overview
provider.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

provider.title: Authentication
provider.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

provider.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
provider.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

provider.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
provider.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

provider.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
provider.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

provider.title: Observability
provider.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

provider.title: Kubernetes Operator
provider.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

provider.title: Supported Providers
provider.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

provider.title: Troubleshooting
provider.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

environments.title: Examples
environments.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

environments.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

filesystem.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
filesystem.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

object.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
object.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

artifact.title: Kubernetes Operator
artifact.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

then.title: Channels
then.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

then.title: A2A Registry
then.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

creates.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
creates.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

apiversion.title: Authentication
apiversion.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

apiversion.title: Deployment with Helm
apiversion.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

apiversion.title: Examples
apiversion.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

apiversion.title: Observability
apiversion.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

information.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

information.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
information.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

spec.title: ADL CLI
spec.excerpt: The ADL CLI is a command-line tool for generating enterprise-ready [A2A (Agent-to-Agent)](/a2a) servers from Agent Definition Language (ADL) YAML files. It eliminates boilerplate code and ensures consistent patterns across your agent implementations, letting you focus on business logic.

spec.title: Deployment with Helm
spec.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

spec.title: Examples
spec.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

spec.title: Observability
spec.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

true.title: ADL CLI

true.title: API Reference
true.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

true.title: Authentication
true.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

true.title: Channels
true.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

true.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration

This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

completion.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
completion.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

completion.title: Troubleshooting
completion.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

health.title: API Reference
health.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

health.title: Inference Gateway CLI
health.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

health.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
health.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

health.title: Kubernetes Operator
health.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

locally.title: API Reference
locally.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

locally.title: Channels
locally.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

locally.title: A2A Registry
locally.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

locally.title: Supported Providers
locally.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

if.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a RESTful API for interacting with language models from various providers.

if.title: Authentication

if.title: Channels

Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

if.title: Deployment with Helm
if.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

if.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
if.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

if.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
if.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

if.title: A2A Registry
if.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

if.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)

settings.title: Authentication
settings.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

settings.title: Inference Gateway CLI
settings.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

settings.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
settings.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

settings.title: Kubernetes Operator
settings.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

settings.title: Troubleshooting
settings.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

want.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
want.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

want.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
want.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

want.title: Kubernetes Operator
want.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

more.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

tool_calls.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
tool_calls.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

general.title: Architecture Overview
general.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

setup.title: Architecture Overview
setup.excerpt: This document provides a high-level overview of the architecture of the Inference Gateway. The Inference-Gateway is designed to be modular and extensible, allowing easy integration of new models and providers.

setup.title: Authentication
setup.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

setup.title: Channels
setup.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

setup.title: Inference Gateway CLI
setup.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

setup.title: Deployment with Helm
setup.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

monitoring.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

monitoring.title: Observability
monitoring.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

monitoring.title: Kubernetes Operator
monitoring.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

configure.title: Channels
configure.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

guide.title: A2A Registry
guide.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

processed.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

unauthorized.title: Authentication
unauthorized.excerpt: Inference Gateway supports authentication through OpenID Connect (OIDC), allowing you to secure your API with various identity providers. This guide focuses on setting up authentication with Keycloak, a popular open-source identity and access management solution.

unauthorized.title: Channels
unauthorized.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

unauthorized.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

returned.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
returned.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). work.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

work.title: Inference Gateway CLI

images.title: Examples
images.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

images.title: A2A Registry
images.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

images.title: Supported Providers
images.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

images.title: Troubleshooting
images.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

fully.title: Observability

before.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

before.title: Inference Gateway CLI
before.excerpt: The Inference Gateway CLI (`infer`) is a powerful Go-based command-line tool providing comprehensive access to the Inference Gateway with interactive chat, autonomous agents, Computer Use tools, and development workflows.

before.title: A2A Registry
before.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

image.title: Channels
image.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

image.title: Examples
image.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

image.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
image.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

image.title: Kubernetes Operator
image.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

image.title: A2A Registry
image.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

image.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
image.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

image.title: Supported Providers
image.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

image.title: Troubleshooting
image.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

will.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

will.title: Integrated Development Environments (IDEs)
will.excerpt: Inference Gateway is fully OpenAI-compatible, allowing you to configure it with your favorite IDEs and extensions using standard OpenAI integration settings. The following sections provide instructions for configuring popular IDEs and extensions.

will.title: Supported Providers
will.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

try.title: Channels
try.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

try.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
try.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

repo.title: Channels
repo.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

repo.title: Deployment with Helm
repo.excerpt: This guide explains how to deploy the Inference Gateway using the official Helm charts. If you'd rather manage gateways and related resources declaratively as Custom Resources, see the <Link href="/operator">Kubernetes Operator</Link>.

repo.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
repo.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

repo.title: A2A Registry
repo.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

ships.title: Channels
ships.excerpt: Channels turn the [Inference Gateway CLI](/cli) agent into a remote-controllable bot accessible from messaging platforms like Telegram. Send a message to your bot and the agent runs as if you launched it locally - with persistent per-sender conversation memory, allowlist-based access control, and optional approval prompts for sensitive tools.

ships.title: Observability
ships.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

ships.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
ships.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

issue.title: Troubleshooting

pull.title: A2A Registry
pull.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

openaigpt4o.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
openaigpt4o.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

openaigpt4o.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
openaigpt4o.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

keys.title: Kubernetes Operator

Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

usage.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
usage.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

usage.title: Observability
usage.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

usage.title: A2A Registry
usage.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. production.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
production.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

production.title: Observability
production.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

production.title: Kubernetes Operator
production.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

production.title: Troubleshooting
production.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

commaseparated.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
commaseparated.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

no.title: Kubernetes Operator
no.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

no.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
no.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

no.title: Supported Providers
no.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

takes.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
takes.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

already.title: A2A Registry
already.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

annotations.title: Kubernetes Operator
annotations.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

examples.title: Examples
examples.excerpt: This page provides examples of how to use Inference Gateway in various scenarios and environments.

examples.title: Getting Started
examples.excerpt: Learn how to install and set up Inference Gateway.

examples.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
examples.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

examples.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
examples.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

examples.title: Observability
examples.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

examples.title: Kubernetes Operator
examples.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

examples.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
examples.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

examples.title: Supported Providers
examples.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

inferaction.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
inferaction.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

notes.title: A2A Registry
notes.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

main.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

cidriven.title: GitHub Action (`infer-action`)
cidriven.excerpt: [`inference-gateway/infer-action`](https://github.com/inference-gateway/infer-action) is the official GitHub Action wrapper for the [`infer` CLI](/cli). It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

yml.title: A2A Registry
yml.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

types.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
types.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

over.title: Inference Gateway Documentation
over.excerpt: Inference Gateway is a proxy server designed to facilitate access to various

over.title: Kubernetes Operator
over.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

over.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
over.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

hit.title: Troubleshooting

Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

It lets you run the Inference Gateway agent from a GitHub Actions workflow so that mentioning a trigger phrase in an issue or comment kicks off an automated, AI-driven response: plan posting, code edits, branch creation, and a pull request - all without leaving GitHub.

author.title: A2A Registry
author.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

exported.title: Observability
exported.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships first-class observability for production deployments: a Prometheus-compatible metrics endpoint with named gateway and tool-call metrics, OpenTelemetry-based instrumentation, structured JSON logs, and reference Grafana / Loki / Jaeger stacks you can copy directly into your cluster.

input.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
input.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

input.title: Troubleshooting
input.excerpt: This page collects the most frequent operational issues hit when running Inference Gateway in production, along with the fastest path to diagnosing and fixing them. Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.

under.title: Kubernetes Operator
under.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

under.title: A2A Registry
under.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

urls.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Integration
urls.excerpt: The Inference Gateway supports **Model Context Protocol (MCP)** integration, enabling seamless access to external tools and data sources for Large Language Models (LLMs). This powerful feature automatically discovers and provides tools to LLMs without requiring clients to manage them individually.

urls.title: A2A Registry
urls.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

registered.title: A2A Registry
registered.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

targets.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
targets.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

orchestrator.title: Kubernetes Operator
orchestrator.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

classname.title: Supported Providers
classname.excerpt: Inference Gateway provides a unified interface to interact with multiple LLM providers.

wraps.title: Software Development Kits (SDKs)
wraps.excerpt: Inference Gateway ships official SDKs for Python, TypeScript, Go, and Rust. Each one wraps the gateway's OpenAI-compatible REST API behind typed clients, handles streaming over Server-Sent Events, and exposes the same provider-agnostic surface (chat, tool calls, vision, MCP).

docs.title: Kubernetes Operator
docs.excerpt: The Inference Gateway Operator is a Kubernetes controller that manages Inference Gateway and related resources declaratively through Custom Resources (CRs). Pick the operator over the <Link href="/deployment">Helm chart</Link> when you want to manage gateways, A2A agents, MCP servers, and chat-channel orchestrators as first-class CRs in the same cluster API your other workloads use.

docs.title: A2A Registry
docs.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

good.excerpt: The **A2A Registry** is the central catalogue for discovering [Agent-to-Agent (A2A)](/a2a) services in the Inference Gateway ecosystem. It's a static web application - hosted at [registry.inference-gateway.com](https://registry.inference-gateway.com) - that lets you browse containerised A2A agents, inspect their metadata, and pull the OCI images you need into your own gateway deployment.

Each entry lists the symptom, the most likely root cause, and a remediation that you can verify against the gateway's logs and metrics.
